agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "SHIP WITH FIXES",
    "one_liner": "Information leakage via 404/403 distinction enables order ID enumeration; missing input validation on orderId allows injection."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "IDOR information leakage via 404/403 oracle",
      "risk": "Attackers can enumerate valid order IDs by distinguishing 404 (doesn't exist) from 403 (exists but not mine). On a public-facing endpoint this leaks business data (order volume, ID scheme) and may enable targeted attacks.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrder handler, lines 7-16 (fetch-then-authorize pattern)",
      "reproduction": "1. Authenticate as any user. 2. GET /orders/{candidateId} for sequential IDs. 3. 403 = valid order belonging to someone else; 404 = no order. 4. Collect all 403 responses to build a map of valid order IDs.",
      "fix": "Query with both orderId AND userId in a single lookup: `db.orders.findOne({ _id: orderId, customerId: userId })`. Return 404 for both not-found and not-authorized. This collapses the oracle into a single indistinguishable response.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authorization model — authenticated users must not be able to infer existence of other users' orders"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation/sanitization on orderId — potential NoSQL/SQL injection",
      "risk": "req.params.orderId is passed directly to db.orders.findById with zero validation. If the DB driver accepts objects (e.g., MongoDB with `{$gt: \"\"}`) or the ID is interpolated into a raw query, this is exploitable for injection or denial of service.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "getOrder handler, line 3 (`const orderId = req.params.orderId`)",
      "reproduction": "1. Send GET /orders/{\"$gt\":\"\"} (URL-encoded). 2. Depending on driver/ORM, this may return the first order in the collection or trigger an error that leaks schema info. 3. For SQL-backed stores, try classic `' OR 1=1 --` payloads.",
      "fix": "Validate orderId format before querying: e.g., for UUID `if (!/^[0-9a-f]{8}-...$/.test(orderId)) return res.status(400)...` or for MongoDB ObjectId use `ObjectId.isValid(orderId)`. Reject anything that doesn't match the expected format.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input validation — all user-supplied parameters must be validated before reaching the data layer"
    },
    {
      "title": "Unauthenticated access when req.user is undefined",
      "risk": "userId is assigned via optional chaining (`req.user?.id`), which yields `undefined` when no auth middleware runs or auth fails silently. The handler continues execution — if any order has a nullish customerId, the `!==` check passes in surprising ways; even without that, the query still hits the database unnecessarily for unauthenticated requests.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrder handler, line 4 (`const userId = req.user?.id`)",
      "reproduction": "1. Send GET /orders/{validId} with no Authorization header. 2. If auth middleware is missing or misconfigured on this route, req.user is undefined. 3. Handler still queries DB and returns 403 (leaking order existence) instead of 401.",
      "fix": "Guard early: `if (!userId) { res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }); return; }`. This should be the first check, before any database access.",
      "time_to_fix": "2 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authentication — unauthenticated requests must be rejected before any data access"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "Full order object returned to client — potential data over-exposure",
      "description": "res.json(order) serializes the entire database record. This likely includes internal fields (createdAt, updatedAt, internal notes, cost breakdowns, other customers' references) that should not be exposed on a public API. Use a DTO/view model to whitelist fields.",
      "location": "getOrder handler, final line (`res.json(order)`)"
    },
    {
      "title": "No error handling around db.orders.findById",
      "description": "If the database call throws (connection timeout, malformed query), the unhandled promise rejection will crash the process or leak a stack trace to the client depending on the error middleware. Wrap in try/catch or ensure a global error handler sanitizes responses.",
      "location": "getOrder handler, line 7"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add rate limiting on this endpoint to mitigate brute-force order enumeration even after fixing the 404/403 oracle.",
    "Add request logging/audit trail for order access — useful for forensics if an authorization bypass is later discovered.",
    "Consider adding an integration test that asserts a user cannot distinguish 'order does not exist' from 'order belongs to another user'."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The IDOR oracle and missing input validation are exploitable immediately on a public-facing endpoint. Deploying as-is exposes the order ID space to enumeration.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — standalone handler, rollback is trivial.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing the 404/403 collapse changes the response code for unauthorized access from 403 to 404. Any client relying on 403 to mean 'order exists but forbidden' will break — but that behavior is itself a security bug, so this is an acceptable breaking change.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics in the handler. Add at minimum: request count by status code, latency histogram, and alert on elevated 4xx rates.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": [
        "403 responses will become 404 after fix — intentional security hardening",
        "Unauthenticated requests will now receive 401 instead of 403/404"
      ]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert the handler to previous version. No database or state changes involved."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 92,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "IDOR oracle is a well-known vulnerability pattern — high confidence this is exploitable",
      "Missing input validation on orderId is directly observable",
      "Optional chaining on req.user without early guard is directly observable"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Injection severity depends on the specific DB driver and ORM in use — could range from non-issue (parameterized queries) to critical (raw interpolation)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "No upstream middleware already validates orderId format or enforces authentication",
      "db.orders.findById does not internally sanitize or parameterize the input"
    ]
  }
}
